Tests the behavior of `Math.min` with `undefined` as an argument.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that uses the `assert` function to verify the behavior of the `Math.min` function when it receives `undefined` as an argument. The test checks that the result of `Math.min(undefined)` is `NaN` (Not-a-Number), which is the expected behavior according to the ECMAScript specification. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific edge case for the `Math.min` function. It is useful for validating the correct implementation of mathematical operations in a JavaScript environment.
